tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i64 @llvm.uadd.sat.i64(i64, i64) #0

define i1 @uadd_sat_uge(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @uadd_sat_uge(
; CHECK-SAME: i64 [[A:%.*]], i64 [[B:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ADD_SAT:%.*]] = call i64 @llvm.uadd.sat.i64(i64 [[A]], i64 [[B]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = and i1 true, true
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP]]
  %add.sat = call i64 @llvm.uadd.sat.i64(i64 %a, i64 %b)
  %cmp1 = icmp uge i64 %add.sat, %a
  %cmp2 = icmp uge i64 %add.sat, %b
  %cmp = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %cmp
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

issue.author: nikic
issue.body:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/4ZG-nD
```llvm
define i1 @src(i64 %index, i64 %limit) {
  %precond = icmp ult i64 %index, %limit
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %precond)
  %sub.sat = call i64 @llvm.usub.sat.i64(i64 %index, i64 1)
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %sub.sat, %limit
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @tgt(i64 %index, i64 %limit) {
  ret i1 true
}
```

I think we should be able to handle this by adding a fact that the usub.sat result is ule its first argument, similar to what we do for min/max/abs.
issue.title: [ConstraintElim] Failure to eliminate cmp of usub.sat

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test(i64 %index, i64 %limit) {
  %precond = icmp ult i64 %index, %limit
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %precond)
  %sub.sat = call i64 @llvm.usub.sat.i64(i64 %index, i64 1)
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %sub.sat, %limit
  ret i1 %cmp
}
